prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of acrolein using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, singlet multiplicity. The geometry was taken from a user-supplied .xyz file and used as-is in a single-point calculation (not re-optimized at DFT). Partial charges computed via Mulliken analysis in PySCF; Fukui indices derived from Mulliken charges on neutral, cation (+1, doublet), and anion (−1, doublet) states. All SCF calculations converged. Charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). The most electrophilic site is atom 4 (terminal alkene carbon, C), with f⁺ = 0.6423; the most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen, O), with f⁻ = 0.3939. Dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) is highest at atom 4 (0.5549), indicating preferential electrophilic attack at that site. A PNG visualization of the Fukui indices was generated. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy = true.
